for_c_level: This isolated test on scenario 18 failed, indicating a high-risk regression in hybrid memory recall that undermines production readiness. Customer-facing claims about seamless AI-to-AI memory sharing are not viable until fixed; this patch likely introduced the issue compared to stable v0.6.2 baseline.
for_non_technical: In this test, two agents shared memories about morning exercise routines, but a third agent only saw one of them when asking about it. This means the system isn't reliably sharing all related memories between agents. It's like a group chat where some messages get lost.
for_sme: Scenario S18 failed with hybrid recall not surfacing Bob's memory (marker bob-daybreak-2ec9df21) in semantic query, as seen_by_charlie=0 despite diag_list presence. Impacts semantic search primitive in federated mesh (W=2/N=4); probable root cause is embedding similarity threshold or quorum read failure in recall mode. Investigate probe F# for hybrid semantics and re-test post-fix.
headline: Hybrid recall failed to surface Bob's memory in semantic query.
next_run_change: Apply fix for semantic query in hybrid mode and expand to full scenario suite for validation.
verdict: FAIL — semantic query missed Bob's memory
what_it_proved: Demonstrated that hybrid recall in semantic mode failed to retrieve Bob's relevant memory, resulting in incomplete sharing visible to Charlie.
what_it_tested: Tested scenario 18 exercising hybrid recall mode with semantic querying across agents in a 4-node federated mesh without TLS, covering recall primitives and federation transport.
